name: Hyperbolic

unions.name: HyperIdealPoint
unions.comment: A hyper ideal point is a point that only exists in the euclidean projection of the hyperbolic plane into the Beltrami–Klein model.

unions.name: IdealPoint
unions.comment: An [ideal point](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ideal_point) is a point at infinity and can be represented by an angle

unions.name: Point
unions.comment:
Module for working with hyperbolic geometry. Uses the Beltrami–Klein model.

Ideal points have Beltrami coordinates with length 1.

Hyper ideal points have Beltrami coordinates with length > 1.

aliases.name: Line
aliases.comment: We define a line by two [ideal points](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ideal_point).

aliases.name: LineSegment
aliases.comment: A line segment is defined by two points

values.name: add
values.comment: Using the Möbius Addition to add two vectors in the poincare disc

values.name: bisectorThrough
values.comment: Construct a bisector through a point

values.name: discFillingPolygon
values.comment:
If you want to construct a tiling in the hyperbolic space, then the length of the polygon specifies how many polygons fit around a point.

If you look up tilings, you will usually find a Schläfli symbol {p,q}. In that case p = vertices, q = polygonsAroundAPoint.

The resulting length is for BeltramiCoords

values.comment: Get the distance between two points

values.name: distanceToOrigin
values.comment: Distance to the origin

values.name: einsteinVelocityAddition
values.comment: Addition for Gyrovectors in the Beltrami Disc Model.

values.name: fromPoincareVector
values.comment: convert a vector into a point

values.name: fromPolarCoords
values.comment: Constructs a point using polar coordinates.

values.name: intersectLines
values.comment: Two lines may intersect at exactly one point

values.name: length
values.comment: Return the length of a vector

values.name: lineFromHyperIdealPointThrough
values.comment: Construct a line using an hyper ideal point

values.name: lineFromIdealPoints
values.comment:
Constructs a line from two ideal points

    lineFromIdealPoints : IdealPoint -> IdealPoint -> Line
    lineFromIdealPoints =
        Tuple.pair

values.name: lineFromLineSegment
values.comment: Convert a line segment into a line

values.name: lineFromPoints
values.comment: constructs a line from two points

values.name: lineSectionTo
values.comment:
Constructs a line segment to a point

    lineSectionTo : BeltramiPoint -> BeltramiPoint -> LineSegment
    lineSectionTo =
        Tuple.pair

values.name: midpointOfLine
values.comment: Construct the midpoint of a line

values.name: midpointOfLineSegment
values.comment: Get the midpoint of a line segment

values.name: nearestIdealPointOf
values.comment: return the nearest ideal point of a line from a given point

values.name: negate
values.comment: Negate a vector

values.name: origin
values.comment: The origin is the point in the center.

values.name: perpendicularLineThrough
values.comment: Point as to lie on the line

values.name: pointAtInfinity
values.comment: An ideal point is a point at infinity, uniquely defined by an angle.

values.name: pointsAlongLine
values.comment: Constructs a given amount of point along a line

values.name: pointsAlongLineSegment
values.comment: Constructs a given amount of point along a line segment

values.name: pointsAreEqual
values.comment: Returns if two point can be considered equal.

values.name: poleOfLine
values.comment: Any line except lines going through the origin, can be associated by a unique hyper ideal point.

values.name: projectFromEuclideanSpace
values.comment:
Projects points from euclidean space into hyperbolic space.
However, you can not expect the proportions to stay the same.

Points further out experience more distortion.

values.name: projectOntoBeltramiKleinDisc
values.comment: Project Hyperbolic points to the Beltrami klein disc

values.name: projectOntoPoincareDisc
values.comment: Project Hyperbolic points to the Poincare disc

values.name: reflectBy
values.comment: reflect a point by a line

values.name: rotateClockwise
values.comment: Rotate the vector clockwise

values.name: rotatePointClockwise
values.comment: Rotate a point clockwise around the origin

values.name: scaleBy
values.comment: scale a vector by a factor

values.name: toPoincareVector
values.comment: convert a point into a vector

values.name: unsafeFromAxialCoord
values.comment: Converts Beltrami Coordinates into [Axial coordinates](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coordinate_systems_for_the_hyperbolic_plane#Axial_coordinates).

values.name: unsafeToAxialCoord
values.comment: Converts [Axial coordinates](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coordinate_systems_for_the_hyperbolic_plane#Axial_coordinates) into Beltrami Coordinates.
